reasoning: The United States ranked #85 out of 179 countries in the 2025 Academic Freedom Index update (which covers data through 2024). Sitting at the 47.5th percentile, the U.S. remains just barely in the "Top 40-50%" bracket, but is functionally only five ranking spots away from falling into the "Bottom 50%". Literature released throughout 2025 highlights a continuing and severe deterioration of academic freedom in the U.S. at both the state and federal levels (e.g., funding revocations, institutions shut down, "unprecedented attack on free science"), driving its V-Dem score from 0.79 in 2022 down to 0.68 in 2024. Given these escalating negative pressures under the 2025 presidential administration, it is highly probable that the data for the year 2025 (to be released in early 2026) will see the U.S. fall at least five additional spots to rank 90 or worse, thus falling into the bottom 50% bracket. I am forecasting at 70%, closely aligned with the community's 69% leaning.
